content:
-
You are a scope-aware ReAct agent. On every turn, emit exactly one block of three lines:
  Thought: <atomic> <free-form reasoning at the atomic level>
  Action: {"name": "<tool_name>", "args": {...}}
  Confidence: <integer 0-100>
The current granularity is atomic. Always prefix the Thought with `<atomic>` so the trajectory logger captures the active scope. To stop, emit an Action with name "Finish" and an "answer" arg.

(Prompt template adapted from the LangChain project (Apache License 2.0). See https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain for the upstream source.)

Problem: Ordering problem in admin.RelatedFieldListFilter and admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter
Description
	
RelatedFieldListFilter doesn't fall back to the ordering defined in Model._meta.ordering. 
Ordering gets set to an empty tuple in ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L196 and unless ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class it stays an empty tuple. IMHO it should fall back to the ordering defined in the related model's Meta.ordering field.
RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter doesn't order the related model at all, even if ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class.
That's because the call to field.get_choices ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L422 omits the ordering kwarg entirely.


Continue with the next Thought / Action / Confidence block.

Problem: Ordering problem in admin.RelatedFieldListFilter and admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter
Description
	
RelatedFieldListFilter doesn't fall back to the ordering defined in Model._meta.ordering. 
Ordering gets set to an empty tuple in ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L196 and unless ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class it stays an empty tuple. IMHO it should fall back to the ordering defined in the related model's Meta.ordering field.
RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter doesn't order the related model at all, even if ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class.
That's because the call to field.get_choices ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L422 omits the ordering kwarg entirely.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- add-metric: Interactively register one or more project metrics in meta/metrics/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a new measurement needs to be tracked across tasks.
- research-internet: Conduct structured internet research and write `research/research_internet.md`.
- setup-remote-machine: Provision, validate, monitor, and tear down remote GPU machines for task execution. Use when a task needs remote machine setup, monitoring, or teardown.
- self-analyze: Analyze a completed Claude Code or Codex session (task execution, brainstorm, project-description, or any other skill invocation) and identify self-corrected mistakes, uncorrected mistakes that affected the result, and human interventions, then propose concrete ARF framework improvements. Prints findings to the chat; does not save reports.
- add-paper: Download a paper, create metadata assets, and register it in the current task.
- human-brainstorm: Run an interactive brainstorming session to review state and choose next actions.
- add-asset-type: Interactively scaffold a new asset type under meta/asset_types/, grounded in project/description.md. Creates a specification.md stub patterned on the built-in asset types. Use during project setup when a custom asset kind is needed; accept "none for now" to skip.
- research-code: Review libraries and prior tasks to write implementation-oriented code research.
- setup-project: One-shot interactive onboarding for a newly forked Glite ARF template. Shows the safety acknowledgement, installs dependencies, runs doctor.py, chains into create-project-description, provisions the paid services the project declared, then invokes the four meta/ sub-skills. Use once per fork, right after cloning.
- compare-literature: Compare task results against published literature and write the comparison output.
- planning: Synthesize research outputs into a task plan with steps, costs, risks, and checks.
- download_paper: Resolve paper identifiers, fetch the PDF and metadata, and prepare inputs for paper asset creation. Use when you need to download a research paper from partial identifiers.
- generate-suggestions: Generate follow-up task suggestions from completed task outputs and project context.
- research-papers: Review downloaded papers and write `research/research_papers.md`.
- self-improvement: Plan and execute a framework change across every related ARF file — specifications, verificators, aggregators, skills, scripts, hooks, tests, materializers, and docs — in a dedicated infrastructure branch and worktree, with style checks and the full test suite. Use whenever anything under `arf/` or generic parts of `meta/` needs to change.
- add-task-type: Interactively add one or more task types to meta/task_types/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a recurring kind of task needs standardized planning and implementation guidance.
- create-project-description: Guide creation of `project/description.md` and `project/budget.json` for a new or revised ARF project. Use when project goals, scope, or budget need to be defined.
- generate-daily-news: Generate Daily News
- execute-task: Run an ARF task through all required stages and merge the final PR.
- implementation: Execute `plan/plan.md`, produce assets, and verify results.
- create-task: Create a new not-started task folder with task.json and task_description.md. Use when any skill or workflow needs to create a new task.
- check-python-style: Check a Python file against the project Python style guide and report violations. Use after editing Python files or when reviewing code style.
- add-category: Interactively add one or more entries to meta/categories/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a new cross-cutting tag is needed.
- check-skill: Verify an ARF skill's structure, shared metadata, symlinks, and markdown quality. Use after creating or updating any skill under `arf/skills/`.
- check-markdown-style: Check a Markdown file against the project markdown style guide and report violations. Use after editing `.md` files or when reviewing markdown quality.
- create-dedup-task: Create a deduplication checkpoint task that scans for duplicate papers and overlapping work. Use when the project needs a new dedup checkpoint.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Thought: <atomic> The current installed version still has the bug - when `NotRegistered` exception is caught, it returns `()` instead of falling back to `field.remote_field.model._meta.ordering`. I need to fix both issues:
1. `RelatedFieldListFilter.field_admin_ordering`: Fall back to `Meta.ordering` when no ModelAdmin is registered.
2. `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter.field_choices`: This is already fixed in the current version (it calls `self.field_admin_ordering`), but the fallback issue in `field_admin_ordering` still exists.

The fix for `field_admin_ordering` should be to return the related model's `_meta.ordering` when no ModelAdmin is registered.

content.content:
# Task Document Rules

## research.md sections

Objective, Background, Methodology Review, Key Findings, Recommended Approach, References.
Full spec: `arf/specifications/research_papers_specification.md`

## plan.md sections

Objective, Approach, Cost Estimation, Step by Step, Remote Machines, Assets Needed,
Expected Assets, Time Estimation, Risks & Fallbacks, Verification Criteria.

## results.md sections

Summary (2-3 sentences with headline metrics), Methodology (machine, runtime, timestamps),
Metrics Tables (per-category + aggregate + stddev), Comparison vs Baselines (always deltas),
Visualizations (embed charts via `![desc](images/file.png)`), Analysis/Discussion, Limitations, Verification,
Files Created, Next Steps/Suggestions.

## Results standards

* Use consistent metrics across tasks for cross-task comparison
* Always include baselines; report improvements as deltas
* Document negative results with the same rigor as positive results
* Include machine specs, runtime, timestamps, worker count for reproducibility
* All charts/graphs saved to `results/images/` and **embedded** in
  `results_detailed.md` with `![description](images/filename.png)` syntax
  so they render visually on GitHub — never just list filenames as text

## Code organization per task

* `paths.py` for centralized `pathlib.Path` constants
* `constants.py` for column names, dtypes, magic strings as typed constants
content.rawContent:
---
paths:
  - "tasks/**"
---

# Task Document Rules

## research.md sections

Objective, Background, Methodology Review, Key Findings, Recommended Approach, References.
Full spec: `arf/specifications/research_papers_specification.md`

## plan.md sections

Objective, Approach, Cost Estimation, Step by Step, Remote Machines, Assets Needed,
Expected Assets, Time Estimation, Risks & Fallbacks, Verification Criteria.

## results.md sections

Summary (2-3 sentences with headline metrics), Methodology (machine, runtime, timestamps),
Metrics Tables (per-category + aggregate + stddev), Comparison vs Baselines (always deltas),
Visualizations (embed charts via `![desc](images/file.png)`), Analysis/Discussion, Limitations, Verification,
Files Created, Next Steps/Suggestions.

## Results standards

* Use consistent metrics across tasks for cross-task comparison
* Always include baselines; report improvements as deltas
* Document negative results with the same rigor as positive results
* Include machine specs, runtime, timestamps, worker count for reproducibility
* All charts/graphs saved to `results/images/` and **embedded** in
  `results_detailed.md` with `![description](images/filename.png)` syntax
  so they render visually on GitHub — never just list filenames as text

## Code organization per task

* `paths.py` for centralized `pathlib.Path` constants
* `constants.py` for column names, dtypes, magic strings as typed constants

content.text:
Thought: <atomic> This is a research task in an ARF framework, not a Django project. The problem statement describes a Django bug, but I need to understand what this task is about. Looking at the step_tracker, this is "phase2_abc_runtime_n147_for_rq1_rq5". The problem statement about Django seems to be the actual software engineering question I need to answer as a ReAct agent. Let me focus on answering the Django ordering problem.

The issue is:
1. `RelatedFieldListFilter.field_admin_ordering` returns `()` when no ModelAdmin is registered, instead of falling back to `Model._meta.ordering`
2. `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` (in Django 2.2.1) doesn't pass ordering to `field.get_choices` at all

The fix should:
1. In `field_admin_ordering`, when `NotRegistered` is raised, return `field.remote_field.model._meta.ordering` instead of `()`
2. In `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter.field_choices`, pass the ordering argument to `field.get_choices`

Now let me find the actual Django source in this repo (if it exists) or determine where to make the fix.
Action: {"name": "Bash", "args": {"command": "find /Users/lysaniuk/Documents/reasoning-scale2-worktrees/t0026_phase2_abc_runtime_n147_for_rq1_rq5 -name '*.py' -path '*/admin/*' 2>/dev/null | head -10"}}
Confidence: 60

content.content:
220	
221	
222	class RelatedFieldListFilter(FieldListFilter):
223	    def __init__(self, field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path):
224	        other_model = get_model_from_relation(field)
225	        self.lookup_kwarg = "%s__%s__exact" % (field_path, field.target_field.name)
226	        self.lookup_kwarg_isnull = "%s__isnull" % field_path
227	        self.lookup_val = params.get(self.lookup_kwarg)
228	        self.lookup_val_isnull = get_last_value_from_parameters(
229	            params, self.lookup_kwarg_isnull
230	        )
231	        super().__init__(field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path)
232	        self.lookup_choices = self.field_choices(field, request, model_admin)
233	        if hasattr(field, "verbose_name"):
234	            self.lookup_title = field.verbose_name
235	        else:
236	            self.lookup_title = other_model._meta.verbose_name
237	        self.title = self.lookup_title
238	        self.empty_value_display = model_admin.get_empty_value_display()
239	
240	    @property
241	    def include_empty_choice(self):
242	        """
243	        Return True if a "(None)" choice should be included, which filters
244	        out everything except empty relationships.
245	        """
246	        return self.field.null or (self.field.is_relation and self.field.many_to_many)
247	
248	    def has_output(self):
249	        if self.include_empty_choice:
250	            extra = 1
251	        else:
252	            extra = 0
253	        return len(self.lookup_choices) + extra > 1
254	
255	    def expected_parameters(self):
256	        return [self.lookup_kwarg, self.lookup_kwarg_isnull]
257	
258	    def field_admin_ordering(self, field, request, model_admin):
259	        """
260	        Return the model admin's ordering for related field, if provided.
261	        """
262	        try:
263	            related_admin = model_admin.admin_site.get_model_admin(
264	                field.remote_field.model
265	            )
266	        except NotRegistered:
267	            return ()
268	        else:
269	            return related_admin.get_ordering(request)
270	
271	    def field_choices(self, field, request, model_admin):
272	        ordering = self.field_admin_ordering(field, request, model_admin)
273	        return field.get_choices(include_blank=False, ordering=ordering)
274	
275	    def get_facet_counts(self, pk_attname, filtered_qs):
276	        counts = {
277	            f"{pk_val}__c": models.Count(
278	                pk_attname, filter=models.Q(**{self.lookup_kwarg: pk_val})
279	            )
